title: Hieracium psilobrachion
intro: Hieracium psilobrachion is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, known for its yellow daisy-like flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of certain conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium psilobrachion include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Herbal preparations made with Hieracium psilobrachion include infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat skin issues, promote wound healing, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Hieracium psilobrachion is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes several species commonly known as hawkweeds. Hieracium psilobrachion specifically belongs to the subgenus Pilosella. The exact classification of this plant may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Pilosella section within the Hieracium genus. Further research may be necessary to provide a more detailed classification.
